value_accrual_summary: All C-Chain and X-Chain transaction fees are burned (reducing supply), while validator/delegator staking rewards are paid from a fixed inflationary emission schedule (capped at 720M AVAX total supply), meaning stakers earn newly minted tokens rather than fee revenue, and LPs/dApp users receive no direct protocol-level fee share.

_notes.burn_detail: All base transaction fees on the X-Chain and C-Chain are burned per the Avalanche whitepaper and confirmed in AvalancheGo source code. P-Chain fees are also burned as of recent upgrades.
_notes.staking_apr_source: CoinGecko public page shows ~8.36% staking APR as of mid-2025; this figure fluctuates with network participation rate (target range ~7–12% per docs). The reward formula in calculator.go confirms rewards are minted from the reserved emission pool, NOT from fee revenue.
_notes.inflation_note: Max supply is capped at 720,000,000 AVAX. As of mid-2025 circulating supply is ~410M; remaining ~310M is reserved for staking rewards (emission). Burn from fees partially offsets but does not eliminate net inflation at current fee volumes.
_notes.fee_capture_caveat: Subnet validators may set custom fee structures for their own subnets, but those are subnet-specific and not part of the primary network value-accrual mechanism described here.
